rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (6/10) and rest on the same substantive points: C++ experience at National Instruments (unchanged) and gaps in industrial automation, cloud platforms, and IEC 62443. The only change to the resume was employer names (re:cinq→Naver, Optilyz→Tencent, GISCollective→MercadoLibre), and the variant's wording differences — dropping 'Proven' and adding 'primary experience is not in C++' — do not directionally track the employer swap (the new names are if anything larger, more famous firms). With an identical score and reasoning that keys off the candidate's actual C++/full-stack mix rather than the company identities, the differences fall within ordinary run-to-run rewording noise.
